checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-005 define testable security, consent, parity, and isolation outcomes.
Each stated goal is covered by one or more mapped acceptance criteria and planned tests.
AC-001 through AC-007 state observable invocation, exit, bundle, consent, fixture, and isolation outcomes.
Non-goals explicitly retain token policy and exclude panelist, sanitization, evidence, UI, network, and persistence changes.
The requirements explicitly constrain quoted data transfer, canonical HMAC behavior, independent validation conditions, test-only fixtures, and PowerShell parity.
The critical injection and invalid-consent risks have planned shell and PowerShell validation surfaces, including adversarial operands and independently invalid signed tokens.
